question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: startBlockNumber] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `startBlockNumber` has no upper-bound validation, so setting it to a very large value causes the importer to skip all currently available blocks, creating a gap in the Hashgraph history that makes the mirror node appear to have no recent transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: endBlockNumber] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@PositiveOrZero` constraint on `endBlockNumber` to set it to 0, causing the importer to immediately stop processing after block 0, truncating the entire Hashgraph block history and making the mirror node serve no block data to downstream consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: endBlockNumber] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between `startBlockNumber` and `endBlockNumber` where no validation enforces `startBlockNumber <= endBlockNumber`, to set `startBlockNumber` greater than `endBlockNumber`, causing the importer to process zero blocks and silently produce an empty Hashgraph history without raising any error?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` is a nullable Long with no validation, so if it is set to 0 or a very early timestamp, the Flyway migration `V1.23.1` sets all `topic_message.running_hash_version` to 2 for all records, causing the mirror node to serve incorrect running hash versions for historical HCS messages and breaking the verifiability of the Hashgraph topic message chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` is used as a raw SQL parameter in `V1.23.1__add_topicmessage_runninghashversion.sql` via `${topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp}`, and if the value is null or malformed, cause the migration to fail or produce incorrect running hash version assignments, corrupting the integrity chain of HCS topic messages in the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a `@NotNull` constraint on `topicRunningHashV2AddedTimestamp` to set it to `Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing the migration to classify all topic messages as using running hash version 1 (pre-v2), making the mirror node serve incorrect running hash versions for all HCS messages and undermining the cryptographic integrity of the Hashgraph topic history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: smartContractThrottlingVersion] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `smartContractThrottlingVersion` is parsed via `Version.parse()` without catching parse exceptions at the property binding level, so supplying a malformed version string causes a startup failure, preventing the importer from processing any Hashgraph stream files and creating a denial-of-history condition?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: dataPath] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `dataPath` defaults to `Paths.get(\

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: dataPath] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `dataPath` is resolved lazily via `@Getter(lazy = true)` for `streamPath`, so if `dataPath` is changed after the first access to `streamPath`, the cached `streamPath` still points to the old location while new stream files are written to the new location, creating a split-brain state in the Hashgraph archive that allows history gaps?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: groupByDay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the boolean `groupByDay` flag to observe that when it is `false`, all stream files are archived to the same flat `streamPath` directory, making it trivial to overwrite older Hashgraph stream files with newer ones of the same name, effectively replacing legitimate historical records with tampered ones?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: groupByDay] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transition from `groupByDay=true` to `groupByDay=false` (or vice versa) mid-operation to cause the importer to archive the same stream file to two different locations, creating duplicate or conflicting Hashgraph history entries that confuse downstream consumers about which version is authoritative?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: importHistoricalAccountInfo] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `importHistoricalAccountInfo` defaults to `true` and controls whether `HistoricalAccountInfoMigration.doMigrate()` runs, to observe that on a mainnet deployment with `startDate` before `2019-09-14T00:00:10Z`, the migration imports pre-reset account data from a bundled classpath resource, and if that resource is tampered with, the attacker can inject false historical account states into the Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: importHistoricalAccountInfo] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between `importHistoricalAccountInfo=true` and `network=mainnet` in `HistoricalAccountInfoMigration` to trigger re-import of historical account info on every repeatable migration run, causing legitimate post-reset account states to be overwritten with pre-reset data from the bundled `accountInfo.txt.gz`, corrupting the entity history in the Hashgraph mirror?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: migration] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `migration` uses a `CaseInsensitiveMap` (from Apache Commons Collections) to inject a migration entry with a key that case-insensitively collides with an existing migration name, causing the wrong `MigrationProperties` to be applied to a critical Hashgraph history migration and potentially disabling or misconfiguring it?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: migration] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@Valid` annotation on the `migration` map to observe that only the values (MigrationProperties objects) are validated, not the keys, allowing injection of arbitrarily named migration entries that could shadow legitimate migration configurations and alter how Hashgraph history migrations are executed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: network] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `@NotBlank` constraint on `network` (which only prevents null/empty/whitespace) to supply a network value like `mainnet-2099-01` that passes validation but causes `getNetworkPrefix()` to return `2099-01`, redirecting block stream discovery to a non-existent or attacker-controlled network folder and causing the importer to ingest forged Hashgraph block streams?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: network] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `network` is not validated against the known set of `HederaNetwork` constants (DEMO, MAINNET, OTHER, PREVIEWNET, TESTNET), so supplying an arbitrary network name causes `getBucketName()` and `getBlockStreamBucketName()` to return empty strings, potentially causing the importer to use a default or misconfigured bucket containing tampered Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: network] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `getNetwork()` strips everything after the first `-` delimiter, so a network value of `demo-anything` is treated as `demo` by `isAllowAnonymousAccess()`, enabling anonymous access to stream files even when the operator intended a non-demo deployment, allowing unauthenticated injection of forged Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: HederaNetwork.isAllowAnonymousAccess()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the exact-match comparison `DEMO.equals(network)` in `isAllowAnonymousAccess()` to observe that only the lowercase string `demo` triggers anonymous access, and craft a network value that `getNetwork()` normalizes to `demo` (e.g., `DEMO`, `Demo`) to enable anonymous stream file access on a deployment that should require authentication, facilitating injection of forged Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: streamPath] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lazy initialization of `streamPath` via `@Getter(lazy = true)` combined with `@EqualsAndHashCode.Exclude` to observe that `streamPath` is excluded from equality checks, allowing two `ImporterProperties` instances with different `dataPath` values to be considered equal, potentially causing the wrong stream path to be used for archiving Hashgraph stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Field: dataPath] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `dataPath` has no `@NotNull` validation beyond the default Lombok `@Data` behavior, so if it is set to a path that does not exist or is not writable, `getArchiveDestinationFolderPath()` returns a path that cannot be written to, causing `Utility.archiveFile()` to fail silently or throw an exception that prevents Hashgraph stream files from being archived and verified?
